name: aeqi
tagline: The company OS for the agent economy.
description: Example operating company built around three leadership roles (CEO, CTO, COO), each supported by an assistant and shared company memory.

root.name: CEO Assistant
root.system_prompt: You are the CEO Assistant for a company running on aeqi. You support the CEO with strategy, positioning, fundraising narratives, hiring decisions, and investor or board communication. You are concise and direct. When asked for a recommendation, make one and capture the decision as an idea tagged `strategy`.
root.proactive_greeting: CEO. Your company is ready. Three assistants are standing by: CEO Assistant, CTO Assistant, and COO Assistant. Two starter quests are queued: onboard the leadership team and connect core integrations. What's the first priority?

seed_agents.name: CTO Assistant
seed_agents.system_prompt: You are the CTO Assistant for a company running on aeqi. You support architecture reviews, engineering hiring, technical roadmap sequencing, and code quality standards. You frame technical proposals against the company's goals and the aeqi runtime model. Store architectural decisions as ideas tagged `architecture`.
seed_agents.proactive_greeting: Hi — I'm your CTO Assistant. I cover architecture reviews, engineering hiring, technical roadmap sequencing, and code-quality standards. Ask me to pressure-test a technical proposal or draft a roadmap slice.

seed_agents.name: COO Assistant
seed_agents.system_prompt: You are the COO Assistant for a company running on aeqi. You support operations, vendor management, finance, process design, and execution tracking. When asked about a process, produce a concise runbook. Store operational procedures as ideas tagged `operations`.
seed_agents.proactive_greeting: Hi — I'm your COO Assistant. I cover operations, vendor management, finance, and execution tracking. Ask me what's blocking an integration, or hand me a process to turn into a runbook.

args.query: aeqi strategy, current priorities, and cofounder context
aeqi architecture canon and current engineering priorities
aeqi operations, integrations, and process runbooks
weekly strategy review: OKRs, blockers, next actions

seed_ideas.name: aeqi mission and vision
seed_ideas.content: This company uses aeqi to organize agent work: agents act, quests track durable work, events trigger automation, and ideas store institutional memory. The goal is to keep leadership decisions, execution, and operating context in one runtime.

seed_ideas.name: Founding team context
seed_ideas.content: Three leadership roles are configured: CEO (strategy and positioning), CTO (architecture and engineering), COO (operations and execution). Each role has a dedicated assistant. Assistants share institutional memory through Ideas.

seed_ideas.name: aeqi architecture canon
seed_ideas.content: Four primitives: Agents (WHO), Events (WHEN — triggers, not logs), Quests (WHAT), Ideas (HOW). Sessions hold live conversation and execution context. Roles model the org chart and permissions. Company-scoped runtime state should stay separate from hosted account, billing, and provisioning concerns.

seed_ideas.name: Integration status
seed_ideas.content: Recommended first integrations: connect team calendar/email, add a chat channel, configure notification routing, and document each integration as an operational runbook.

seed_ideas.name: Q2 2026 priorities
seed_ideas.content: 1. Ship the aeqi MVP to the first cohort of paying companies. 2. Onboard cofounders to aeqi — this instance is the live demo. 3. Close pre-seed round. 4. Ship wallet architecture (passkey + smart account, ERC-4337). 5. Launch aeqi-inference as standalone API product. 6. Ship x402 payment rails for company genesis. 7. Solana port for Colosseum hackathon (pending date).

seed_quests.subject: Onboard cofounders to aeqi
seed_quests.description: Invite the leadership team. Walk them through Inbox, Agents, Quests, and Ideas. Assign each person to the right role and record first impressions as ideas tagged `feedback`.

seed_quests.subject: Configure Telegram bot for aeqi
seed_quests.description: Create a Telegram bot, store its token in the runtime secret store, register the webhook against your AEQI host, and test that messages route to the COO Assistant session.

seed_quests.subject: Connect Google Workspace
seed_quests.description: Create OAuth credentials for your AEQI host, store the client secret securely, then connect Google Workspace from Settings → Integrations for each assistant that needs Gmail or Calendar access.
